ai_videos.note: daily_ai_videos array has exactly 1,481 entries. Monthly summary sums to 1,481. Spot-checked 3 months (Jan 2025=3, Aug 2025=69, Mar 2026=334) -- all match monthly_summary claims. Entries span 2025-01-06 through 2026-04-02.

creators.note: The daily_ai_videos array (2025+) contains only 433 unique channels. The claimed 815 comes from creator_influence_map.total_unique_creators which covers the full 3-year arc (Apr 2023-Apr 2026, 2,215 videos). The 815 figure is for a different scope (all-time, not 2025+). This is a scope conflation, not a fabrication, but the headline number is misleading if presented as a 2025+ figure.

ai_searches.note: youtube-search-analysis.json has exactly 618 entries in ai_coding_searches. However, 4 of those are tagged only as 'creative' or 'tools' (not 'ai'): 'procedural Loop generated recursive' (creative), and 3 'kilo code' searches (tools). If 'AI searches' means entries tagged 'ai', the count is 614, not 618. Minor semantic ambiguity.

total_searches.note: youtube-search-history.json has exactly 6,813 raw entries. youtube-search-analysis.json total_searches field also says 6,813. Monthly distribution sums to 6,813. Date range 2017-09-04 to 2026-03-30 confirmed.

details.channel: The Late Show with Stephen Colbert
details.notes: Severance TV show interview. Contains zero AI content. The word 'Severance' may have triggered a false match if the classifier uses keyword matching on related terms. This is a clear false positive.

agent_arch_keyword_confusion.description: Videos about government/law enforcement 'agents' (ICE, FBI, TSA, federal agents) classified as 'agent_arch' (AI agent architecture). These are immigration/crime news videos, not AI content.
affected_entries: "Pay These People!" - Wanda Sykes Shows Some Love For The TSA Agents (2026-03-13)
MS NOW EXCLUSIVE: FBI agents concerned Kash Patel is purging staff (2026-03-06)
Newsom says Trump sending federal agents to San Francisco (2025-10-23)
California Will Now Require ICE Agents To Show Their Faces (2025-09-24)
LIVE: Federal agents conduct immigration operation in Camarillo (2025-07-10)
Viewed A federal judge ordered the Trump administration to release... (news item)
ICE Agents Cosplay As Rogue Cops (2025-06-20)
Trump mobilizes Marines to protect and transport ICE agents (2025-06-10)
agent_arch_keyword_confusion.impact: 8 out of 1,481 entries (0.5%) are clear keyword-based false positives in agent_arch. Does not significantly affect totals but indicates automated classification without channel-aware filtering.

entertainment_as_ai_content.description: Colbert late-night clips containing 'AI' in a subtitle segment (e.g., 'AI Wants To Nuke Us', 'AI Product Placement') classified as AI content. These are entertainment monologues where AI is a brief comedic segment, not substantive AI engagement.
entertainment_as_ai_content.impact: Low. Colbert clips represent ~14 entries total. Some genuinely mention AI. Only the Severance interview (0 entries with actual AI content) is a definitive false positive.

duplicate_entries.description: 63 videos appear more than once (re-watched on different dates). Only 2 titles appear twice on the same date: 'AI Agent Learns to Escape' (2026-03-20 x2) and 'AI Olympics' (2026-03-21 x2). These could be data artifacts or legitimate double-watches.
duplicate_entries.verdict: Re-watches on different dates are legitimate behavioral data. Same-date duplicates are minor anomalies (2 entries out of 1,481).

quiet_period_correction.details: The quiet_period_correction section correctly notes that the 'quiet period' claim of 0 commits applies only to Liminal, not to other repos. It identifies 13 commits across 4 other repos on March 27. The correction is applied transparently and does not alter the core narrative -- it adds nuance.

methodology_gap.description: No methodology documentation exists for how videos were classified into topics (coding_agents, llm_watched, agent_arch, other_ai, ml_fundamentals, local_ai, ai_general, creative_coding, model_routing, ai_fundamentals). The false positives in agent_arch (government 'agents') strongly suggest automated keyword matching was used, likely matching on 'agent' without disambiguating law enforcement from AI. No classification rubric, confidence scores, or manual review notes were found.
methodology_gap.recommendation: Add a methodology section to youtube-ai-correlation.json documenting: (1) whether classification was manual, LLM-assisted, or keyword-based; (2) the rubric for each topic category; (3) known limitations; (4) estimated false positive rate.

data_quality_issues.issue: Creator count scope conflation: 815 unique creators is for all-time (3 years, 2,215 videos), not the 2025+ scope (1,481 videos, 433 unique channels). The creator_influence_map.total_unique_creators field and the 2025+ daily_ai_videos have different scopes but the 815 number appears alongside the 1,481 video count without clarification.

Undocumented topic classification methodology. The presence of keyword-based false positives (government 'agents' in agent_arch, Severance interview as other_ai) indicates automated classification without human review or channel-aware filtering.
data_quality_issues.affected_entries: 8 confirmed false positives in agent_arch, 1 in other_ai

data_quality_issues.issue: AI search count ambiguity: 618 total entries in ai_coding_searches, but only 614 are tagged 'ai'. The other 4 are tagged 'tools' (3 kilo code searches) and 'creative' (1 procedural loop search). If 'AI coding searches' requires the 'ai' tag, the count should be 614.

2 same-date duplicate entries: 'AI Agent Learns to Escape' and 'AI Olympics' both appear twice on the same date. These could be data ingestion artifacts (YouTube history logging the same watch twice) or legitimate re-watches within the same day.

The 'other_ai' category is a catch-all (498 entries, 34% of total). It includes everything from Colbert monologues to AI industry news to gadget reviews. This broad classification reduces the analytical value of the topic breakdown.

overall_assessment.summary: The core counts (1,481 AI videos, 6,813 searches, 618 AI searches) are accurate. Monthly breakdowns are internally consistent. The data is real Google Takeout export data with genuine YouTube watch/search history. However: (1) the 815 creator count is for a different scope than the 1,481 video count, (2) topic classification has ~0.6% clear false positives from keyword confusion, and (3) no classification methodology was documented. The data is suitable for narrative purposes but should not be used for precise quantitative claims without noting the scope and classification limitations.
